content:
# Registration Renewal Eligibility

This document describes the eligibility check flow for vehicle registration renewal in the transport portal, including system interactions and decision logic.

## Sequence Diagram

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor Customer
    participant Portal
    participant Database
    participant PaymentGateway

    Customer->>Portal: Submit renewal request (plate + DOB)
    Portal->>Database: Validate vehicle ownership
    Database-->>Portal: Owner record returned
    Portal->>Database: Check outstanding fines
    Database-->>Portal: Fine status: clear
    Portal->>Database: Check vehicle inspection status
    Database-->>Portal: Inspection: current
    Portal->>Portal: Calculate renewal fee
    Portal->>Customer: Display fee summary + confirm
    Customer->>Portal: Confirm payment
    Portal->>PaymentGateway: Initiate payment
    PaymentGateway-->>Portal: Payment authorised
    Portal->>Database: Update registration record
    Portal->>Customer: Send confirmation + new certificate
```

## Eligibility Decision Table

| Check | Condition | Pass | Fail |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Identity | Plate matches owner DOB | Continue | Block — identity mismatch |
| Outstanding fines | No unpaid infringement notices | Continue | Block — must clear fines first |
| Inspection status | Current roadworthy certificate | Continue | Block — inspection required |
| Registration status | Not cancelled or suspended | Continue | Block — contact transport office |
| Payment | Card authorised | Issue certificate | Retry or cancel |

## Notes

- All eligibility checks run in **parallel** where data dependencies allow, reducing latency
- Failed checks return a specific error code so the portal can display targeted remediation steps
- The payment step only proceeds when all prior checks pass; partial completion is not permitted
- Certificates are generated as signed PDFs and stored for 7 years per retention policy
